title: Interest Rates and Equity Valuation
summary: Higher interest rates tend to reduce present values of future cash flows and can pressure growth stock valuations.

title: Inflation and Consumer Demand
summary: Persistent inflation can change spending behavior as households prioritize essentials over discretionary goods.

title: Volatility and Risk Management
summary: Volatility should be sized against position risk so portfolio losses remain survivable under stress.

title: Momentum Strategies in Practice
summary: Momentum strategies often buy recent winners and sell laggards but require strict risk controls during reversals.

title: Moving Averages as Trend Filters
summary: Short and long moving averages help identify trend direction and reduce noise in day to day price action.

title: RSI and Mean Reversion Signals
summary: RSI can highlight overbought and oversold zones but should be combined with broader trend context.

title: Drawdowns and Capital Preservation
summary: Maximum drawdown quantifies peak to trough loss and helps evaluate strategy durability.

title: AI Sector Growth Narrative
summary: AI driven productivity expectations can support technology sector multiples when earnings execution follows.

title: Earnings Expectations and Repricing
summary: Price often reacts more to the gap between reported results and expectations than to absolute numbers.

title: Diversification Across Assets
summary: Diversification reduces concentration risk by combining assets with different return drivers and correlations.

title: Liquidity Conditions and Risk Appetite
summary: Loose liquidity can increase risk taking while tighter liquidity often compresses valuations.

title: Behavioral Biases in Markets
summary: Anchoring and herd behavior may amplify trend persistence or panic selling around key events.
